summary: PR 2573 made a focused data-matching pass in the Inishie1 stage translation unit, changing only src/melee/gr/grinishie1.c. The diff populated grI1_803E4950 stage-data callback entries that had been NULL, adjusted report/assert string literals, stopped using a separate grI1_803E49A8 file-name string in an OSReport call, and changed one assert message from new_gobj to map_gobj. The bot report recorded .data improvement for main/melee/gr/grinishie1 from 55.52% to 89.65% and a tiny grInishie1_801FB3F0 score improvement.

key_files.role: Only changed file; Inishie1 stage source containing grI1_803E4950 stage data, report/assert strings, and related callbacks.

what_changed.text: The invalid_state macro OSReport arguments changed from file-name literal grinishie1.c plus line to the string i!=HATENA_APPEAR_CHECKLOOP plus line, and the report format gained the newline shown in the diff.

grI1_803E4950 stage data had several NULL initializer slots replaced with concrete symbols: grI1_803E48F4, grInishie1_801FA90C, grInishie1_801FA908, grInishie1_801FA984, grInishie1_801FA988, grInishie1_801FA9AC, grInishie1_801FCBB0, and grInishie1_801FCBB8.
The separate char array grI1_803E49A8[] = "grinishie1.c" was commented out, and grInishie1_801FA9B4 now passes the inline literal "grinishie1.c" to OSReport.
The assert in grInishie1_801FB3F0_inline changed its message from "%s:%d: oioi..." to the newline-containing form shown in the diff.
The null-gobj assert in fn_801FBF6C changed its message string from "new_gobj" to "map_gobj".

smart_moves.text: Kept the PR tightly scoped to a single stage TU and a small number of data/string initializer edits.
Used inline file-name string literal in the OSReport call rather than retaining a separate address-named char array for the same text.
Populated existing stage-data initializer slots with already-named functions instead of introducing new wrapper symbols or broad source restructuring.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For stage data matching, callback-table initializer slots can be important data-section evidence; replacing placeholder NULLs with existing callback symbols may substantially improve .data matching when the table ownership is known.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grI1_803E4950 NULL entries replaced with callback symbols

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not assume a separate address-named storage symbol is needed for a file-name string used in reporting; this PR improved matching while changing an OSReport call to use the inline literal "grinishie1.c".
Small punctuation, newline, and exact assert/report text changes can be part of data matching; preserve exact strings shown by evidence rather than normalizing them for readability.

naming_conventions.text: This PR retained existing address-style and subsystem names such as grI1_803E4950, grI1_803E48F4, and grInishie1_801FA90C rather than inventing new semantic names during data matching.
The assert message changed from "new_gobj" to "map_gobj" in fn_801FBF6C, but the PR evidence does not explain the naming rationale beyond the changed literal.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Data initializer matching: fill grI1_803E4950 callback slots with existing function/data symbols instead of leaving NULL placeholders.
String-literal placement tactic: remove practical use of an address-named file-name char array by inlining "grinishie1.c" at the OSReport callsite.
Exact assert/report text matching: adjust message strings, including newline-containing strings, to improve data layout or string matching.

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev[bot] reported 2 improvements in unmatched items for GALE01: main/melee/gr/grinishie1 .data improved by +139 bytes from 55.52% to 89.65%, and grInishie1_801FB3F0 improved from 80.43% to 80.44% with 0 bytes listed.
No human review comments were present in the loaded PR slice.

follow_up_queries: grI1_803E4950 stage data callback initializer NULL grInishie1
grinishie1.c grI1_803E49A8 inline string literal OSReport

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR number, title, author, merge state, changed file counts
raw_diff: complete 2359-byte diff for one file
text_corpus: empty PR body and one bot report
notes: The diff is complete and the bot report gives concrete matching-score evidence, so file-level and score-change facts are strong.
The PR body is empty and there are no human review comments, so author intent, rationale for each string, and reviewer acceptance reasoning are not evidenced beyond the merge metadata and bot report.
The slice does not include local objdiff output beyond the decomp-dev bot summary; do not claim full match or absence of regressions.
The commented-out char array remains visible in the diff, so this intake describes practical removal from use rather than asserting final cleanup of all dead code.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Stage-data callback table entries can drive data matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2573, grI1_803E4950 in src/melee/gr/grinishie1.c improved .data matching after NULL callback slots were replaced with existing grInishie1/grI1 symbols. Curator should treat this as a localized lesson for stage data initializer recovery, not a blanket rule to fill NULLs without table evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Inline ordinary report file-name literals unless data ownership evidence requires storage
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2573 changed an OSReport call from passing grI1_803E49A8 to passing the inline literal "grinishie1.c" and the bot report showed data-match improvement. This supports the existing standards that ordinary report strings should not be promoted to address-named storage without ownership evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: grinishie1.c owns Inishie1 stage data initializer grI1_803E4950
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2573 changed src/melee/gr/grinishie1.c to improve main/melee/gr/grinishie1 .data matching by editing grI1_803E4950, including stage-data callback entries and report format strings.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful localized path fact for future curator search; requires curator verification against current source before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Exact assert/report strings may be data-match significant
source_update_candidates.text: Consider whether existing assert/report and literals standards should explicitly mention preserving exact punctuation and newline content in report/assert strings when evidence shows data-match impact.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: invalid_state string changed to newline-containing form
source_update_candidates.reason: This PR supplies one example, but it is too narrow to promote automatically as a new global standard.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all NULLs in stage-data initializers should be replaced; this PR only evidences specific grI1_803E4950 slots.
Do not claim grInishie1_801FB3F0 or the full TU matched; the bot report shows only a small score improvement and no full-match claim.
Do not infer reviewer rationale or human approval comments; none were loaded.
Do not promote the exact string changes as generally semantically correct beyond the evidence that they were part of this merged data-matching PR.
